meta.siteDescription: Shoa Lin's personal site about AI agents, knowledge systems, developer tools, and technical writing.

theme.label: Theme
theme.toggle: Switch between light and dark themes

home.intro: I focus on AI agents, knowledge systems, and developer tools, especially how tool use, memory, evaluation, and workflows come together in real tasks.
home.supporting: This site collects my articles, research notes, and long-term reading list.
home.interestsBody: I study how agents can use tools reliably, retain context, recover from failure, and leave results that people can review.
home.backgroundBody: My work has involved knowledge graphs, data, and enterprise architecture. I now spend most of my time on AI agent systems and developer workflows.
home.approachBody: I check the real state first, break the problem down, test assumptions, and turn the result into reusable code or writing.
home.contactBody: Email or find me on X to discuss AI agents, knowledge systems, developer tools, and technical writing.

about.title: About
about.paragraphOne: My work has involved knowledge graphs, data, and enterprise architecture. I now spend most of my time on AI agent systems and developer workflows.
about.paragraphTwo: I care not only about what a model can answer, but also how it uses tools, retains context, handles failure, and leaves work that can be reviewed.
about.backgroundBody: That experience shaped my interest in system boundaries, information structure, and turning complex work into verifiable steps.
about.interestsBody: Tool use, memory, evaluation, workflows, knowledge organization, and how they combine in real tasks.
about.principleEvidenceBody: For runtime state, code behavior, or product decisions, I return to real files, logs, and test results.
about.principleClarityBody: Separate verified facts, reasonable inferences, and what remains unknown.
about.principleUsefulBody: Research should end in something executable, reviewable, or ready for the next iteration.

blog.title: Writing
blog.description: Long-form notes on AI agents, knowledge systems, developer tools, and engineering practice.
blog.empty: There are no articles in this category yet.

favorites.title: Favorites

favorites.empty: No saved items match this filter yet.
